front: What does Azure RBAC use to define WHO can do WHAT on WHICH scope?
back: A role assignment = security principal + role definition + scope. Permissions are additive across assignments.

front: Difference between Azure RBAC roles and Microsoft Entra roles?
back: RBAC roles control access to Azure resources (management plane). Entra roles control identity/directory tasks (users, groups, app registrations).

front: What is a dynamic membership group and what license does it need?
back: A group whose members are added/removed automatically by attribute-based rules. Requires Microsoft Entra ID P1.

front: What are the four built-in fundamental RBAC roles?
back: Owner (full + manage access), Contributor (full but no access mgmt), Reader (view only), and User Access Administrator (manage access only).

front: Where can a management group be placed in the hierarchy?
back: Above subscriptions. Management groups can contain subscriptions and other management groups; policy/RBAC inherit downward.

front: What is a managed identity?
back: An Entra identity for an Azure resource (system- or user-assigned) so it can authenticate to services without stored credentials.

front: Two types of resource locks and their effect?
back: CanNotDelete (modify allowed, delete blocked) and ReadOnly (only read; no modify or delete). Most restrictive wins.

front: What Azure Policy effect deploys a missing related resource automatically?
back: deployIfNotExists. Others include deny, audit, append, modify, and auditIfNotExists.

front: List the storage redundancy options from least to most resilient.
back: LRS (1 datacenter) → ZRS (3 zones) → GRS (2 regions async) → GZRS (zones + region) → RA-GRS/RA-GZRS add read on secondary.

front: What are the three blob access tiers?
back: Hot (frequent access), Cool (infrequent, ≥30 days), Archive (rare, ≥180 days, offline — must rehydrate to read).

front: What is a stored access policy used for?
back: A container-level policy that SAS tokens reference, allowing revocation/change of many SAS tokens without rotating account keys.

front: Account SAS vs service SAS?
back: Account SAS covers one or more services at account level. Service SAS is scoped to a single service (blob/file/queue/table), often via a stored access policy.

front: Which protocol does Azure Files support for Windows and Linux mounting?
back: SMB (Windows/Linux/macOS) and NFS (Linux, premium accounts). Blob storage is REST/object, not SMB.

front: What does customer-managed account failover do for a GRS account?
back: Promotes the geo-secondary region to primary so you can write to it during a prolonged primary-region outage.

front: Which account kind is recommended for most new workloads?
back: General-purpose v2 (GPv2) — supports all services and access tiers. Use BlockBlobStorage/FileStorage for premium performance.

front: What enables fully private connectivity to a storage account over a private IP?
back: A private endpoint via Azure Private Link. Service endpoints secure traffic but still use the public endpoint.

front: Availability set vs availability zone?
back: Availability set spreads VMs across fault/update domains in one datacenter. Availability zones are physically separate datacenters in a region (higher SLA).

front: What does a VM Scale Set add over an availability set?
back: Identical VM instances with autoscale (scale in/out by metrics or schedule), plus zone and fault-domain spreading.

front: Difference between ACI and AKS?
back: ACI runs single/few containers serverlessly (burst, simple jobs). AKS is managed Kubernetes for orchestrating many containers at scale.

front: What are App Service deployment slots used for?
back: Staging environments with warm-up and zero-downtime swaps; can route a % of traffic for testing. Requires Standard tier or higher.

front: ARM deployment modes: incremental vs complete?
back: Incremental (default) adds/updates template resources, leaves others alone. Complete deletes resources in the group not in the template.

front: What is Bicep?
back: A declarative DSL that transpiles to ARM JSON, with simpler syntax, modules, and type safety for infrastructure as code.

front: Which disk types exist and which can't be an OS disk?
back: Standard HDD, Standard SSD, Premium SSD, Premium SSD v2, Ultra Disk. Ultra Disk and Premium SSD v2 cannot be OS disks.

front: Two ways to run a script on a VM after deployment?
back: Custom Script Extension and Run Command (plus cloud-init for Linux at provisioning).

front: What is the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler in AKS?
back: It scales the number of pod replicas based on CPU/memory metrics. The Cluster Autoscaler scales the number of nodes.

front: VNet peering key facts?
back: Connects VNets over the Azure backbone using private IPs, low latency, non-transitive by default. Can be regional or global.

front: How are NSG rules evaluated?
back: By priority, lowest number first; first match wins. Default rules allow VNet/LB and deny inbound internet.

front: How many IPs does Azure reserve per subnet?
back: 5 (network, gateway, two for Azure DNS, broadcast). A /27 (32) leaves 27 usable.

front: Azure Load Balancer vs Application Gateway vs Traffic Manager?
back: Load Balancer = L4 regional. Application Gateway = L7 regional (URL routing, WAF, TLS). Traffic Manager = DNS-based global routing.

front: Service endpoint vs private endpoint?
back: Service endpoint extends VNet identity to a PaaS public endpoint (still public IP). Private endpoint gives the PaaS a private IP in your subnet.

front: What does Azure Bastion provide?
back: Secure browser-based RDP/SSH to VMs over TLS without exposing public IPs or opening 3389/22 to the internet.

front: What does a NAT gateway solve?
back: Scalable, predictable outbound internet connectivity for a subnet with static public IPs, reducing SNAT port exhaustion.

front: Site-to-site VPN vs ExpressRoute?
back: S2S VPN: encrypted tunnel over public internet. ExpressRoute: private, dedicated circuit via provider that bypasses the internet.

front: Traffic Manager routing methods?
back: Priority, Weighted, Performance, Geographic, MultiValue, and Subnet.

front: What is a Log Analytics workspace?
back: Central store for logs/metrics queried with KQL; target of the Azure Monitor Agent and diagnostic settings.

front: Metric alert vs Activity Log alert?
back: Metric alert evaluates numeric platform/guest metrics vs thresholds. Activity Log alert fires on control-plane events (create/delete/etc.).

front: What is an action group?
back: A reusable set of notifications and actions (email, SMS, webhook, Logic App, runbook, function) triggered by alerts.

front: Azure Backup vs Azure Site Recovery?
back: Backup = point-in-time recovery points with retention (uses Recovery Services vault). ASR = replication and failover for DR.

front: What restore options does Azure VM Backup offer?
back: Create new VM, restore disks, replace existing disks, and file-level (item-level) recovery.

front: What do diagnostic settings configure?
back: Which platform logs/metrics are collected and where they are routed: Log Analytics, storage account, and/or Event Hub.
